turns.note: Forward corruption x-tilde = x_0 + eps, eps ~ N(0, sigma^2 I) matches Eq 1.1, manifold intuition and MSE loss (Eq 1.2) both from the notes; source_mode file.

Tweedie stated exactly as Eq 1.3 (E[x|x-tilde] = x-tilde + sigma^2 grad log P) with the notes' where-you-are/direction/step-size intuition and an accurate Combo B citation; the aside that direct prediction and score matching are 'mathematically equivalent' slightly overstates the notes.
Reparameterization written exactly as Eq 2.1 (z = mu_phi(x) + sigma_phi(x) elementwise eps, eps ~ N(0,I)), pieces identified correctly, no DAE mixing.
Gives the notes' reason verbatim in spirit — decouples random sampling from deterministic parameters so backprop can occur — tied to the Turn 3 formula, no ELBO drift.
Summary sheet carries all three formulas character-for-character plus the why-backprop reason; student-appropriate and nothing from Turns 1-4 lost.

Exactly four questions appended with the sheet unchanged, but Q4 conflates concepts (asks how the reparameterization trick helps a DAE-trained score model 'learn a good representation'), which the sheet does not support and is not cleanly answerable from it.
Reissues the student sheet with the four questions and no printed answers; reference formulas remain as study material, content otherwise unchanged.

highlight: All three VAE-notes formulas (DAE corruption, Tweedie, reparameterization) were preserved exactly through seven turns of edits; the only weak spot is self-check Q4, which mixes DAE score training with the VAE reparameterization trick in a way the sheet cannot answer.
